title: Security, Privacy, Reliability, and Layered Quality Evidence · Claim Audit: Threat Abuse Case
summary: A product passes a familiar unit test but trusts input, overcollects data, leaks failure details, loses work, and has no threat or recovery evidence. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp1 threat abuse case increment in trust and quality assurance case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Threat Abuse Case · Theory Threat Abuse Case
steps.instruction:
theory case — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Threat Abuse Case contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Threat Abuse Case · Theory Threat Abuse Case.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk. For Predict Threat Abuse Case · Theory Threat Abuse Case, keep Threat Abuse Case connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. content.label: Authority to consult after committing

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Threat Abuse Case while producing trust and quality assurance case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The worked example variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk. A defensible solution applies Threat Abuse Case, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trust and quality assurance case.

content.code:
# Worked focus: pp1-threat-abuse-case — Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-5
focus: pp1-threat-abuse-case
decision-evidence: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.text: Security review is a vulnerability scanner run after coding. This fails the Threat Abuse Case contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

stimulus.title: Threat Abuse Case failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Security review is a vulnerability scanner run after coding.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

options.text: Reconstruct the conditions for Threat Abuse Case, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

Keep the Threat Abuse Case artifact unchanged and rewrite its explanation so the unsupported output appears intentional. That claim fails the repair the threat abuse case defect evidence boundary for Threat Abuse Case.

For repair the threat abuse case defect, apply Threat Abuse Case without its stated preconditions, omit the trust and quality assurance case trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Threat Abuse Case for trust and quality assurance case.

steps.title: Defend the Threat Abuse Case model · Theory Threat Abuse Case
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Threat Abuse Case: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A familiar happy-path test passes while hostile input, duplicate action, partial write, privacy deletion, dependency timeout, and restart remain unexamined. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.

steps.why: Retrieving Threat Abuse Case through the concrete trust and quality assurance case case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Defend the Threat Abuse Case model · Theory Threat Abuse Case, keep Threat Abuse Case connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Model assets, actors, trust boundaries, misuse, abuse, failure impact, mitigations, detection, response, and residual risk.
